global_objective: Make the exec CLI refuse to record state or commit a delegated task without a captured agentId (single and --batch), unless the gap is explicitly acknowledged, and document the contract in plan-executor SKILL.md.

tasks.justification: Bounded implementation of one guard on an existing CLI code path with clear pass/fail ACs (R1.S1/S2/S3) — sonnet fits.
tasks.instructions: Implement R1 in the single-task complete command of the exec CLI. Before any state write or git commit, guard as R1.S2 requires: a delegated task (plan entry declaring an agent_type) closed with neither an agent id nor the acknowledgment flag must be refused via the canonical {ok:false,error:{reason}} envelope whose reason begins 'MISSING_AGENT_ID:' and names the task_id, non-zero exit, leaving execution_state.json and git log unchanged. Keep R1.S1 (id supplied → records agentId, exit 0, existing verification outcome unchanged) and R1.S3 (the acknowledgment flag from the spec's fixed naming → pre-existing graceful degrade: agentId null, incidencia contains the reason, exit 0). Honor the Technical Requirements: stdout carries only the {ok,...} envelope, the guard runs before any state write or commit, no new dependencies. Follow the exact flag naming fixed in the spec's Assumptions.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Modified exec CLI single-task complete command enforcing R1, plus its tests, passing.

test_contract.assertion: Completing a delegated task with an agent id supplied exits 0 and its state entry records that id as agentId.

Completing a delegated task with neither an agent id nor the acknowledgment flag exits non-zero, error.reason begins 'MISSING_AGENT_ID:' and names the task_id, and both the state file and git log are unchanged from before the call.
Completing a delegated task with the acknowledgment flag carrying a reason exits 0, records agentId null and an incidencia containing that reason.

tasks.justification: Bounded implementation extending the same guard to the batch path with clear all-or-nothing ACs (R2.S1/S2/S3) — sonnet fits.
tasks.instructions: Implement R2 in the complete --batch command, reusing the guard added in T1-single-complete-guard. Apply R1's rule per entry up front, all-or-nothing, matching the batch's existing pre-commit guards: R2.S2 — any entry with neither an agent id nor its per-entry acknowledgment field rejects the whole batch via {ok:false,error:{reason}} with reason beginning 'MISSING_AGENT_ID:' naming the offending task_id, non-zero exit, nothing recorded or committed. R2.S1 — every entry carrying an id succeeds ({ok:true,data:{status:'batch',...}}, exit 0, each entry records its id). R2.S3 — an entry carrying the per-entry acknowledgment field (snake_case, per the spec's fixed naming) records agentId null with incidencia containing the reason while the rest record their ids. Honor the same Technical Requirements as T1 (envelope-only stdout, guard before any write/commit, no new deps).
tasks.expected_output_schema: Modified exec CLI complete --batch command enforcing R2 all-or-nothing, plus its tests, passing.

test_contract.assertion: A batch where every entry carries an agent id exits 0 and each entry's state records its id.

A batch with one entry lacking both an agent id and the acknowledgment field exits non-zero with reason beginning 'MISSING_AGENT_ID:' naming that task_id, and nothing in the batch is recorded or committed.

A batch mixing one acknowledged entry (reason supplied) with id-carrying entries exits 0; the acknowledged entry records agentId null plus an incidencia containing the reason, and the rest record their ids.

tasks.justification: Prose update of the plan-executor SKILL.md contract following a fixed schema (R3.S1); doc_writer/sonnet fits, no code.
tasks.instructions: Update the plan-executor SKILL.md §2/§3 contract per R3.S1: state that complete and complete --batch reject a delegated task with no captured agent id, and document the explicit acknowledgment escape hatch (the single-task and batch acknowledgment forms fixed in the spec's Assumptions), instead of presenting the agent-id flag as advisory. Keep it consistent with the behavior implemented in T1-single-complete-guard and T2-batch-complete-guard. AC7 is verified by human judgment that the prose is accurate and coherent.
tasks.expected_output_schema: Updated plan-executor SKILL.md §2/§3 documenting the block and the acknowledgment escape hatch.

tasks.justification: R-E2E.S1 is a reject-then-record sequence on one task that no per-task test chains end-to-end, so the integration test must be authored (code_writer per e2e-task-assignment guidance).
tasks.instructions: Author the integration test for R-E2E.S1 exercising the guards from T1-single-complete-guard and T2-batch-complete-guard as one sequence against a validated plan with a delegated task on a fresh execution_state.json: complete once with green evidence but no agent id (must exit non-zero, reason beginning 'MISSING_AGENT_ID:', leaving no state entry and no commit), then re-complete with an agent id and the same green evidence (state entry becomes status 'done' with the supplied agentId and a commit exists). Assert the commit-presence transition across the two calls, not just the isolated per-task behaviors.
tasks.expected_output_schema: New integration test covering the R-E2E.S1 reject-then-record sequence, passing.

test_contract.assertion: The reject-then-record sequence leaves no commit on the first (blocked) call and yields a done state entry carrying the supplied agentId plus a commit after the second call.
